text:
Australia — Investment Screening Mechanism profile (PRISM ISM structured data, snapshot year 2023).
Context: mechanism first established in 1975; member of OECD, Five Eyes.
Number of distinct mechanisms: 3.
Coverage: Mixed.
Notification requirement: mandatory for some transactions.
Pre-approval: mandatory for some transactions.
Lead authority: Foreign Investment Review Board.
Review timeframe: 220 days.
Ownership threshold: 10%.
Mechanism features — Formal call-in power: Yes; Greenfield investments covered: Yes; Real estate covered: Yes; Reviews increases in existing ownership: Yes; Filing fees: Yes; Mitigation agreements: Yes; Fines for non-compliance: Yes; National-security / public-order test: Yes; Net-benefit test: Yes; Competition test: No; Inter-agency review: Yes.
Explicitly covered sectors/technologies: Enhanced review of Foreign Gov't Control, Defense Production, Energy Infrastructure, Water Infrastructure, Transportation Infrastructure, Telecommunications Infrastructure, Healthcare Infrastructure, Agriculture/Food Security, Finance, Media, Sensitive Personal Data, Defense Technologies, Energy Storage, Mineral Resources, Next Gen Critical Infrastructure, Finance, Media, Access to Personal Sensitive Data.
Recent legislative changes — 2020: lowered monetary thresholds to zero; 2021: increases critical infrastructure to 11 sectors; call-in power; mandatory notification; any business over $2B = critical infrastructure asset; 2021: increased timeline and added greenfield; 2022: increases threshold for media sector; 2022: Requires annual reports for critical infrastructure assets and special reporting for cybersecurity incidents; 2022: doubles foreign investment application fees and penalties.

Recent legislative changes — 2011: establishes national security related screening; 2013: extends timelines; 2020: establishes interagency review board, changes thresholds, and adds sectors; replaced 2011.
